awards.title: Uber Technology Award
awards.summary: Peer recognition for zero-downtime Elasticsearch-to-Apache Pinot migration and CRM messaging reliability improvements.

awards.title: Uber Velocity Award
awards.summary: Peer recognition for vouchers on-call reduction program and cross-team platform delivery velocity.

awards.title: Excellence Award
awards.summary: Given in recognition of outstanding delivery of our Total Cloud Data Center provisioning and network automation solution.

basics.name: Shane Ray
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer | Distributed Systems & Applied AI
basics.summary: Senior Software Engineer with 5+ years at Uber as primary backend owner for the Uber for Business vouchers platform: Go microservices for billing, trip lifecycle, CRM messaging, and production on-call. Backend and full-stack IC on Kafka-backed distributed systems, a 9-month zero-downtime Elasticsearch-to-Apache Pinot migration, and enterprise-scale reliability programs. Applied AI practitioner: agent workspaces, prompt-driven codemods adopted across teams (850+ test files), and cross-service RCA tooling. Prior technical lead for a 10,000+ account B2B SaaS platform.
basics.summaryShort: Senior Software Engineer with 5+ years at Uber owning Go microservices for Uber for Business billing, trip lifecycle, and vouchers at enterprise scale. Primary backend on-call; led 9-month zero-downtime Elasticsearch-to-Apache Pinot migration and cut CRM email failures from 17% to under 0.5%. Applied AI practitioner: agent workspaces and prompt-driven codemods (850+ files) adopted across teams. Prior technical lead for 10,000+ account B2B SaaS.
basics.summaryKeywords: Go, Golang, microservices, distributed systems, Kafka, Elasticsearch, Apache Pinot, Redis, SQL, CI/CD, Bazel, React, TypeScript, system design, on-call, SRE, incident response, RCA, Applied AI, LLM, prompt engineering, scalability, backend engineer

projects.name: IC agent workspace (super-shane)
projects.description: Node.js agent workspace for vouchers on-call RCA, diff authoring, and structured memory with hook-governed tool dispatch.
highlights: Single dispatcher over registered tools; tiered pre-flight context (~55% lower bootstrap token use vs unstructured chat sessions, measured internally).
Task-runner dashboard, mail/docs/ticket ingest, conventions catalog, and deterministic investigation capture.
Presented at internal Eng AI Happy Hour (May 2026) as IC plus agent pairing for production engineering.

projects.name: AI-driven Enzyme test stack migration
projects.description: React/TypeScript: led Uber for Business Vouchers deprecation of 850+ legacy Enzyme test files using prompt-engineered, review-gated codemods.
highlights: Methodology adopted by Global Support Services and other teams after IC handoff.
Preserved CI, lint, and coverage gates while reducing manual migration labor.

projects.name: Go Interface Shadow Runner
projects.description: Go library for live shadow traffic during Elasticsearch-to-Apache Pinot datastore cutovers with built-in migration dashboards.
highlights: Used during vouchers Elasticsearch-to-Apache Pinot migration (Apr 2023-Jan 2024) with built-in migration dashboards.
Designed for reuse by other teams performing datastore cutovers.

references.name: Phil Scott (Senior Architect)
references.reference: Shane is a tireless worker and learner. He not only looks to constantly improve his work, but also take what he's learned and share it with others.

references.name: Ashish Arora (Manager Software Development)
references.reference: Shane is an excellent software developer. He takes a great deal of pride in what he does and he works very hard to achieve the exact results that the customer is looking for. He has worked many late hours as part of my development team just to make certain that a delivered project is exactly what the customer wanted. Shane is passionate about writing code and solving complex problems through development of creative and innovative software. He is conceptually very strong and stays up-to-date with the latest techniques and tools related to software development.

references.name: Shannon Callaway (Cloud Technology Strategist)
references.reference: Shane was a fantastic employee. He was an extremely dedicated worker who consistently worked to improve his skill set and rise to new challenges. He was personable, dependable, and able to quickly adapt to new situations.

work.summary: Primary backend IC and on-call owner for Uber for Business vouchers: Go microservices for billing, trip lifecycle, admin web app, and CRM transactional messaging at enterprise scale.
highlights: Reduced voucher transactional email failure rate from 17% to under 0.5% via CRM template and observability fixes adopted platform-wide for Uber for Business enterprise customers.
Led 9-month zero-downtime Elasticsearch-to-Apache Pinot migration for vouchers analytics using Go shadow-traffic library; pattern reused for other datastore cutovers.
Drove ~50% on-call alert reduction across vouchers Go microservices; documented GSS handoff saving 120+ engineer-hours; Uber Technology and Velocity peer awards.
Built agent workspace for on-call RCA and diff authoring (~55% lower LLM bootstrap tokens vs unstructured sessions); presented at Uber Eng AI forum (May 2026).
Led AI-assisted deprecation of 850+ Enzyme test files across vouchers admin codebase; methodology adopted by Global Support Services and other teams.
Drove Uber Me Quick Send backend, server-driven UI paths, geolocation autoclaim, and web-vouchers performance rehaul (50+ memoized selectors).

work.summary: Contract engineer on Uber Transit (Jun-Nov 2021) then Uber for Business Vouchers (Dec 2021-May 2022); converted to full-time May 2022.
Technical lead owning B2B SaaS platform serving 10,000+ accounts: customer portal, self-service marketplace, CI/CD across 4 environments, and .NET Core migration.
highlights: Architected and delivered customer account portal replacing legacy system serving 10,000+ B2B accounts.
Built CI/CD and deployment automation across 4 environments; led .NET Core migration reducing deployment friction.
Technical lead for 6 engineers on architecture, code quality, and delivery practices.
Led system integration across 2 acquisitions for knowledge transfer and platform consolidation.
Designed and built online marketplace enabling self-service ordering for new telecom customers.
Identified and resolved technical debt across 3 major product lines, improving release velocity.

work.summary: Member of the core services team responsible for partnering and interacting with technical and business contacts to transform, enhance, and build efficiencies.
highlights: Created architecture and developed new backend payment processor to replace legacy payment integration.
Migrated core libraries and services to .NET Core/.NET Standard, reducing deployment friction.
Partnered with DevOps to expand automated testing and release pipeline for core WCF payment services.
Mentored junior and senior developers on architecture, code quality, and delivery practices.

work.summary: Helped lead efforts of migration of company wide primary B/OSS legacy WCF client / server application to Web API and AngularJS system.
highlights: Helped lead migration of company-wide primary B/OSS legacy WCF application to Web API and AngularJS.
Implemented automated continuous delivery using TeamCity, Jenkins, and Octopus Deploy.
Worked with Senior Architect to establish REST API best practices and Swagger documentation.
Maintained primary business portal for internal employees and customers (C#, ASP.NET, JavaScript).
Managed and onboarded geographically diverse teams to meet delivery dates during acquisition integration.
Conducted developer training on unit testing, GitFlow, message queuing, and productivity practices.
Performed code reviews ensuring security, performance, and quality standards on line-of-business code.
Worked with offshore teams to close documentation gaps and improve knowledge transfer.
Used Git Flow via Atlassian JIRA and Stash.
Focused on security and performance.

work.summary: Cloud engineer building network and virtualization management APIs for provisioning, logging, and automation across Fortinet, Juniper, Cisco, Palo Alto, and VMware.
highlights: Designed and maintained network management API for Fortinet, Juniper, Cisco ASA/NSX, and Palo Alto devices.
Designed and maintained VMware vSphere/Orchestrator API for VM provisioning, management, and data retrieval.
Developed and maintained APIs and tools for fellow cloud engineers.

work.summary: Responsible for creating and re-defining processes for support staff.  Responsible for Tier 3 Support escalations.  Continued to develop tools and re-work systems to make Tier 2 Support teams and Tier 3 support more efficient, productive, and happy.  Took on new product development working to integrate tools, processes, and added automation to provide a better customer experience.  Worked heavily with System Center, Hyper-V manager, Fortinet Fortigate 3950B and FortiOS 4.11, PowerShell, Cisco UCS, and others.
highlights: Took initiative to develop tools and re-work systems to make Tier 2 Support teams more efficient, productive, and happy.

work.summary: Tier 2 Support Engineer for cloud hosting products including but not limited to IIS, WebDAV, FTP, MSSQL, MySQL, dedicated and virtual hosted servers.
highlights: Took initiative to develop tools and re-work systems to make Tier 2 Support teams more efficient, productive, and happy.

work.summary: Answered calls for AppleCare CPU tech support. I was responsible for asking relevant probing questions and analyzing the information the customer provided.  Then I would isolate the customers issue to hardware or software and offer the best possible resolution.
highlights: I provided solutions to many networking, security, and DNS issues to maintain productivity on the call floor.  Also made some tools to help advisers through their day to increase productivity and limit distractions.  I have also identified many security holes and provided patches or fixes for them.

SykesLinks – Custom home page that puts all adviser tools in one place.  Also includes a simplified Kronos (time card).  This also gives the supervisor easy access to see which Kronos code that the advisers were in.
Apple Support Sites – The apple support websites that advisers use often suffered from a DNS issue that caused loading times to reach 5+ min.  Identified problems, found solution and provided patch.
Provided excellent customer service and often received support member of the week based on customer satisfaction surveys.
Worked with many different aspects of the Apple computer.  Such as, but not limited to: Email, Networking Issues (wired, wireless), and Hardware/Software Issues

work.summary: Developed and edited many complex websites with ASP.NET, C#, PHP, MySQL, HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
highlights: Designed Graphics to make the overall site design more appealing.
Optimized Code and graphics for faster loading.
Customer Relations, to make sure the customer gets what they need when they needed it.

work.summary: Inspected and repaired various Sanyo/Sprint cellphones down to component level.
highlights: Soldering on a daily basis
Worked as a member of the Quality Control team
